html:
[2024]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 遂設竒兵待之 - 遂 means 'then' or 'thereupon,' 設竒兵 refers to setting up a clever or unexpected military strategy, and 待之 means 'to await them.' This phrase indicates a strategic military maneuver.
  • 有蕃中酋帥衣绯茸裘系寳装带 - 衣 means 'wearing,' 衿茸裘 refers to a red fur coat, 系 means 'wearing,' and 寳装带 refers to a belt adorned with precious decorations. This describes a prominent leader from a foreign tribe dressed in luxurious attire.
  • 频召汉军鬭 - 频 means 'frequently,' 召 means 'calling,' 汉军 means 'Han army,' and 鬬 means 'to engage in battle.' This indicates that the foreign leader frequently challenged the Han army to fight.
  • 白公诫兵士无得而应之 - 白公 refers to Bai Gong (a person's name), 诫 means 'warned,' 兵士 means 'soldiers,' 无得 means 'not to,' and 应之 means 'respond to it.' This shows that Bai Gong warned his soldiers not to respond to the challenges.
  • 俄而驻军指挥背我师百余步而立 - 俄而 means 'soon,' 驻军指挥 means 'commander of the stationed troops,' 背 means 'facing away from,' 我师 means 'our army,' and 百余步 means 'more than a hundred steps.' This describes the commander positioning himself facing away from the Han army.
  • 有潞州小骁勇善射驰马弯弧而出 - 有 means 'there was,' 潞州小 refers to a small unit from Lushan, 骁勇 means 'brave and valiant,' 善射 means 'skilled in archery,' 驰马 means 'riding a horse,' 弯弧 means 'drawing the bow,' and 出 means 'to emerge.' This describes a brave and skilled archer from Lushan.
  • 连发两箭皆中项跃马而前 - 连发 means 'firing consecutively,' 两箭 means 'two arrows,' 皆中 means 'both hit,' 项 means 'neck,' 跃马 means 'leaping on the horse,' and 前 means 'forward.' This describes the archer hitting the target twice and then leaping forward on his horse.
  • 抽短剑踣于鞍上以手扶挟如鬭殴之状 - 抽 means 'drawing,' 短剑 means 'short sword,' 踣 means 'falling,' 于鞍上 means 'on the saddle,' 以手扶挟 means 'using hands to support,' and 如鬭殴之状 means 'in the manner of fighting.' This describes the archer drawing a short sword and falling on the saddle, using his hands to support himself in a fighting posture.
  • 蕃军但呼噪助之于鞍脱绯裘解金带夺马而还 - 蕃军 means 'foreign troops,' 但 means 'only,' 呼噪 means 'shouting and cheering,' 助之 means 'to help him,' 于鞍 means 'from the saddle,' 脱 means 'removing,' 绯裘 means 'red fur coat,' 解 means 'untying,' 金带 means 'golden belt,' and 夺马而还 means 'seizing the horse and returning.' This describes the foreign troops shouting and cheering, helping the archer remove his coat and belt, and then seizing the horse and returning.
  • 师旅无不奋勇 - 师旅 means 'troops,' 无不 means 'all,' and 奋勇 means 'brave and courageous.' This indicates that all the troops were brave and courageous.
  • 既而大战沙漠虏阵瓦解 - 既而 means 'then,' 大战 means 'great battle,' 沙漠 means 'desert,' 虏阵 means 'enemy formation,' and 瓦解 means 'disintegrating.' This describes a great battle in the desert where the enemy formation disintegrated.
  • 所获驼马辎重不可胜计 - 所获 means 'captured,' 驼马 means 'camels and horses,' 辎重 means 'supplies,' and 不可胜计 means 'too many to count.' This indicates that the Han army captured a vast amount of camels, horses, and supplies.
  • 束手而降四三万人 - 束手 means 'surrendering,' 而降 means 'and submitting,' and 四三万人 means '43,000 people.' This describes 43,000 people surrendering.
  • 先是河湟郡界在匈奴者自此悉为唐土 - 先是 means 'previously,' 河湟郡界 means 'the border regions of the Hexi and Huangshui prefectures,' 在匈奴者 means 'those under the control of the Xiongnu,' 自此 means 'from this point on,' 悉 means 'all,' and 为唐土 means 'became part of the Tang territory.' This indicates that the border regions previously controlled by the Xiongnu became part of the Tang territory.
  • 宣宗初览防书云我知敏中必殄凶丑 - 宣宗 refers to Emperor Xuanzong, 初 means 'initially,' 览防书 means 'reading the report,' 云 means 'said,' 我知敏中 means 'I know that Minzhong,' and 必殄凶丑 means 'will surely destroy the wicked and ugly.' This describes Emperor Xuanzong reading the report and saying that he knew Bai Gong would surely destroy the enemy.
  • 白公凯旋与同列宰相进诗云一诏皇城四海颁丑戎无数束身还 - 白公 refers to Bai Gong, 凯旋 means 'returning in triumph,' 与同列宰相 means 'with his fellow chancellors,' 进诗 means 'presenting a poem,' 一诏 means 'one edict,' 皇城 means 'imperial city,' 四海 means 'all over the world,' 颁 means 'issued,' 丑戎 means 'wicked barbarians,' 无数 means 'innumerable,' and 束身还 means 'returning bound.' This describes Bai Gong returning in triumph with his fellow chancellors and presenting a poem that says one edict from the imperial city was issued to all over the world, and the innumerable wicked barbarians returned bound.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蕃中酋帅 - Refers to a prominent leader from a foreign tribe.
  • 绯茸裘 - A red fur coat, indicating luxury and status.
  • 寳装带 - A belt adorned with precious decorations, indicating wealth and status.
  • 白马骏异无比 - A highly distinguished white horse, symbolizing nobility and excellence.
  • 锋镝未交 - Weapons not yet engaged, indicating the pre-battle phase.
  • 频召汉军鬭 - Frequent challenges to the Han army, indicating the enemy's confidence and aggression.
  • 白公诫兵士无得而应之 - Bai Gong warning his soldiers not to respond, indicating strategic caution.
  • 俄而驻军指挥背我师百余步而立 - The commander positioning himself facing away from the Han army, indicating a tactical move.
  • 有潞州小骁勇善射驰马弯弧而出 - A brave and skilled archer from Lushan, indicating the presence of skilled warriors.
  • 连发两箭皆中项跃马而前 - The archer hitting the target twice and then leaping forward, indicating exceptional skill.
  • 抽短剑踣于鞍上以手扶挟如鬭殴之状 - The archer drawing a short sword and falling on the saddle, using his hands to support himself in a fighting posture.
  • 蕃军但呼噪助之于鞍脱绯裘解金带夺马而还 - The foreign troops shouting and cheering, helping the archer remove his coat and belt, and then seizing the horse and returning.
  • 师旅无不奋勇 - All the troops were brave and courageous.
  • 既而大战沙漠虏阵瓦解 - A great battle in the desert where the enemy formation disintegrated.
  • 所获驼马辎重不可胜计 - The Han army captured a vast amount of camels, horses, and supplies.
  • 束手而降四三万人 - 43,000 people surrendered.
  • 先是河湟郡界在匈奴者自此悉为唐土 - The border regions previously controlled by the Xiongnu became part of the Tang territory.
  • 宣宗初览防书云我知敏中必殄凶丑 - Emperor Xuanzong reading the report and saying that he knew Bai Gong would surely destroy the enemy.
  • 白公凯旋与同列宰相进诗云一诏皇城四海颁丑戎无数束身还 - Bai Gong returning in triumph with his fellow chancellors and presenting a poem that says one edict from the imperial city was issued to all over the world, and the innumerable wicked barbarians returned bound.

[2025]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 戍楼吹笛人休战牧野嘶风马自闲 - A complex phrase combining multiple images into a single sentence, describing a scene of peace and tranquility.
  • 西边北塞今无事为报东南夷与蛮 - Elliptical sentence requiring careful parsing to understand the relationship between the peaceful borders and the message to the southern and eastern tribes.
  • 指挥文武皆神算恢拓乾坤是圣功 - Uses parallelism to describe the strategic and divine nature of the rulers' actions.
  • 天留此事还英主不在他年在大中 - Elliptical sentence requiring careful parsing to understand the timing and significance of the wise ruler's appearance.
  • 戎虏乞降归惠化皇威渐被慑腥羶 - Elliptical sentence requiring careful parsing to understand the relationship between the submission of the barbarians and the imperial authority.
  • 穹庐逺戍烟尘灭神武光竹帛传 - Elliptical sentence requiring careful parsing to understand the relationship between the peace and the recording of history.
  • 左袵尽知謌帝泽从兹不更备三边 - Elliptical sentence requiring careful parsing to understand the relationship between the barbarians' knowledge of the emperor's benevolence and the peace of the borders.
  • 崔相诗云边陲万里注恩波宇宙群方洽凯歌 - Mix of narrative and poetic expression, requiring careful translation to maintain the flow and meaning.
  • 烟尘永息三秋戍瑞气遥清九折河 - Describes the permanent cessation of war and the end of the autumn garrison, along with the auspicious atmosphere and the clarity of the winding river.
  • 共偶圣明千载运更观俗阜与时和 - Refers to the appearance of a wise ruler in a thousand-year cycle and the observation of prosperity and harmony with the times.
  • 【出剧谈录】 - Indicates that the source of this text is the *Ju Tan Lu* (Record of Dramatic Conversations).

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 犬戎 - A nomadic group that historically inhabited the western regions of China.
  • 河陇 - Refers to the regions around the Yellow River and the Gansu corridor, which were strategically important in ancient China.
  • 四帅 - Likely refers to four prominent generals or military leaders.
  • 英主 - Refers to a wise and capable ruler.
  • 大中 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Taizong, who is known for his wise and benevolent rule.
  • 魏相扶 - Likely refers to a historical figure, possibly a minister or advisor.
  • 秦原 - Refers to the Qin region, which was an important area in ancient China.
  • 戎虏 - Refers to barbarian tribes that were often in conflict with the Chinese empire.
  • 皇威 - Refers to the imperial authority and power.
  • 穹庐 - Refers to the tents of nomadic tribes.
  • 神武 - Refers to divine martial prowess.
  • 竹帛传 - Refers to the recording of history in written form.
  • 左袵 - Refers to the barbarians on the left (east) side of the empire.
  • 三边 - Refers to the three borders of the empire.
  • 崔相 - Likely refers to a historical figure, possibly a minister or advisor.
  • 边陲 - Refers to the border regions of the empire.
  • 宇宙群方 - Refers to the entire universe and all directions.
  • 凯歌 - Refers to victory songs.
  • 三秋戍 - Refers to the autumn garrison, a period of military preparation.
  • 瑞气 - Refers to auspicious or lucky signs.
  • 九折河 - Refers to the winding river, symbolizing the complexity and twists of life.
  • 共偶圣明千载运 - Refers to the appearance of a wise ruler in a thousand-year cycle.
  • 更观俗阜与时和 - Refers to the observation of prosperity and harmony with the times.

[2026]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 舉進士 - Refers to taking the imperial examination for the jinshi degree, a key route to officialdom in the Tang dynasty.
  • 語亦不正 - Means 'speech is also not standard,' referring to non-standard or unusual speech patterns.
  • 呼防為慧葢 - A nickname given to Lu Fang, likely due to a speech impediment or unusual pronunciation.
  • 舌短也 - Refers to a speech impediment, possibly a lisp or difficulty pronouncing certain sounds.
  • 昆弟 - Refers to brothers, but can also mean close relatives or associates.
  • 輕侮之 - Means 'to mock or belittle,' indicating disdain or disrespect.
  • 加敬 - Means 'to show respect' or 'to treat with deference.'
  • 人物甚陋 - Means 'personality is very poor' or 'character is lacking,' suggesting that Lu Fang's character or appearance was not impressive.
  • 觀其文章有首尾 - Means 'observe that his writings have a beginning and an end,' implying coherence and structure in his work.
  • 卜之 - Means 'to predict' or 'to foretell,' suggesting that the outcome was anticipated based on signs.
  • 必為大用乎 - Means 'will certainly be of great use,' indicating a prediction of future success.
  • 竟登廊廟 - Means 'eventually reached the highest offices,' referring to holding high-ranking positions in the imperial court.
  • 福建觀察使 - Refers to the governor of Fujian, a significant administrative position.
  • 率不展分 - Means 'generally did not show respect,' indicating that the brothers did not treat Lu Fang well.
  • 以貌失人 - Means 'to lose someone because of their appearance,' a common saying indicating that one should not judge by appearances.
  • 諸季 - Refers to younger brothers or relatives, emphasizing the contrast between the brothers' behavior and the outcome for Lu Fang.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐丞相盧防 - Lu Fang, a high-ranking official during the Tang dynasty.
  • 大中初 - Refers to the early years of the Dazhong era (847-859 AD), a period in the Tang dynasty.
  • 進士 - The highest degree in the imperial examination system, indicating a high level of scholarly achievement.
  • 慧葢 - A nickname given to Lu Fang, possibly due to a speech impediment or unusual pronunciation.
  • 韋氏昆弟 - Refers to the brothers of the Wei family, a prominent family during the Tang dynasty.
  • 廊廟 - Refers to the imperial court, indicating high-ranking positions.
  • 福建觀察使 - A significant administrative position in the Tang dynasty, responsible for overseeing the province of Fujian.
  • 北夢瑣言 - A historical text that records anecdotes and stories from the Tang and Five Dynasties periods.
  • 唐令公韋昭度 - Wei Zhaodu, a high-ranking official during the Tang dynasty.
  • 少貧窭 - Means 'young and poor,' indicating that Wei Zhaodu came from a humble background.
  • 人倫之鑒 - Means 'insight into human nature,' suggesting that Master Jingguang had a keen understanding of people's character.
  • 器重之 - Means 'to value highly,' indicating that Master Jingguang held Wei Zhaodu in high regard.
  • 摭言 - A historical text that records anecdotes and stories from the Tang dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 盧防 - Lu Fang, a high-ranking official during the Tang dynasty.
  • 韋岫 - Wei Xiu, a brother of the Wei family who later respected Lu Fang.
  • 韋昭度 - Wei Zhaodu, a high-ranking official during the Tang dynasty.
  • 福建觀察使 - The governor of Fujian, a significant administrative position in the Tang dynasty.
  • 北夢瑣言 - A historical text that records anecdotes and stories from the Tang and Five Dynasties periods.
  • 摭言 - A historical text that records anecdotes and stories from the Tang dynasty.

[2027]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 尚书 - High-ranking official title in the Tang dynasty, often translated as 'Minister' or 'Chancellor.'
  • 知人之鉴 - Means 'the ability to discern people's character or potential,' often translated as 'insight into human nature' or 'judgment of character.'
  • 俾其子叔向以下备酒馔于山亭 - Complex phrase meaning 'ordered his son Shuxiang and others to prepare wine and food at the mountain pavilion.'
  • 召二顾赐宴 - Means 'summoned the two Gus for a banquet.'
  • 俄亦即席 - Means 'soon after, he also took his seat.'
  • 约令勿起 - Means 'ordered them not to rise.'
  • 惶惑莫谕其意 - Means 'they were confused and did not understand his intention.'
  • 公弟兄俱有封侯之相 - Means 'both of you have the appearance of being destined to be marquises.'
  • 善自保爱 - Means 'take good care of yourselves.'
  • 他年愿以子孙相依 - Means 'in the future, I hope to rely on your descendants.'
  • 洎黄寇犯阙 - Means 'when the Yellow Bandits invaded the imperial palace.'
  • 顾彦朗领本军同立复功 - Means 'Gu Yanlang led his troops to help restore order.'
  • 除东川加使相 - Means 'was appointed governor of Dongchuan and given the title of '使相' (a high-ranking official).'
  • 请叔向为节度副使 - Means 'asked Shuxiang to be the deputy governor.'
  • 仍以丈人行拜之 - Means 'and treated him as a father figure.'
  • 军府大事皆谘谋焉 - Means 'all major military and administrative matters were discussed with him.'
  • 大顾薨其弟彦晖嗣之亦使相 - Means 'when the elder Gu died, his younger brother Yanhui succeeded him and also held the title of '使相'.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蔡荆尚书 - Refers to Cai Jing, a high-ranking official in the Tang dynasty.
  • 黄寇 - Refers to the Yellow Bandits, a group of rebels during the Tang dynasty.
  • 东川 - Refers to Dongchuan, a region in the Tang dynasty.
  • 使相 - A high-ranking official title in the Tang dynasty, often translated as 'Governor with the rank of Chancellor.'
  • 节度副使 - Refers to the deputy governor of a military region.
  • 丈人行拜之 - A cultural practice where a younger person treats an elder as a father figure, showing respect and deference.

Proper Nouns

  • 蔡荆 - Cai Jing, a high-ranking official in the Tang dynasty.
  • 顾彦朗 - Gu Yanlang, a low-ranking official or servant.
  • 黄寇 - The Yellow Bandits, a group of rebels during the Tang dynasty.
  • 东川 - Dongchuan, a region in the Tang dynasty.
  • 使相 - A high-ranking official title in the Tang dynasty.
  • 节度副使 - The deputy governor of a military region.
  • 丈人行 - A cultural practice where a younger person treats an elder as a father figure.

[2028]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 欲知囚情 - To know the prisoner's situation, used to express the intention to understand the circumstances of the prisoners.
  • 以梧槚为人 - To use camphor trees as people, a metaphorical expression meaning to create wooden figures representing prisoners.
  • 凿地为陷 - To dig the ground to make a pit, referring to the creation of a pit in the ground.
  • 以芦为郭 - To use reeds to make a wall, indicating the use of reeds to create a boundary around the pit.
  • 卧木囚其中 - To place wooden prisoners inside, meaning to put the wooden figures inside the pit.
  • 囚罪正是木囚不动 - If the prisoner's crime is correct, the wooden prisoner does not move, indicating that the wooden figure remains still if the prisoner is guilty.
  • 囚寃侵夺木囚动出 - If the prisoner is wronged and invades the wooden prisoner, the wooden prisoner moves out, indicating that the wooden figure moves if the prisoner is innocent.
  • 不知囚之精神着木人邪 - Not knowing whether the prisoner's spirit is on the wooden figure, expressing uncertainty about whether the prisoner's spirit is affecting the wooden figure.
  • 天神之气动木囚也 - The movement of the wooden prisoner is due to the divine breath, suggesting that the movement is caused by divine intervention.
  • 会楚王坐事平相牵引 - When the king of Chu was involved in a case and others were implicated, indicating that the king of Chu was involved in a legal case, and others were implicated.
  • 拘系者千余人 - Thousands of people were detained, indicating a large number of people were detained.
  • 毒楚横暴 - The cruelty and violence of Chu, describing the harsh treatment of the prisoners.
  • 囚皆自诬 - The prisoners all falsely confessed, indicating that the prisoners were coerced into confessing to crimes they did not commit.
  • 坐掠幽而死者百余人 - Over a hundred people died from torture and imprisonment, indicating the severe consequences of the legal proceedings.
  • 天用炎旱赤地千里 - The sky used intense drought to turn a thousand miles of land red, describing the severe drought.
  • 安授拜即控辔而行 - An accepted his appointment and immediately took control of the reins and proceeded, indicating that An took immediate action.
  • 既到决狱事人人具录辞状 - Once he arrived, he resolved the cases, and each person recorded their statements, indicating that An resolved the cases and documented the prisoners' statements.
  • 本非首谋为王所引 - They were not the main conspirators but were implicated by the king, indicating that the prisoners were not the primary culprits but were implicated by the king.
  • 应时理遣 - They were promptly released, indicating that the prisoners were quickly freed.
  • 一日之中延千人之命 - In one day, he saved the lives of a thousand people, indicating the significant impact of An's actions.
  • 其时甘雨滂霈 - At that time, it rained abundantly, indicating the relief brought by the rain.
  • 岁大丰稔 - The year was a great harvest, indicating a bountiful year.
  • 行部闻道旁女子哭而声不哀 - While traveling, he heard a woman crying beside the road, but her voice was not sad, indicating that the woman's crying was unusual.
  • 问之亡夫遭烧死 - He asked her, and she said her husband had been burned to death, indicating the woman's explanation for her crying.
  • 遵敕吏舆尸到 - Zun ordered the officials to bring the body, indicating that Zun ordered the officials to bring the body.
  • 令人守之 - He ordered someone to guard it, indicating that someone was assigned to watch over the body.
  • 当有物往更日有蝇聚头所 - There should be something coming, and after a day, flies gathered around the head, indicating the presence of a supernatural phenomenon.
  • 考问以淫杀夫 - He questioned her about killing her husband with adultery, indicating the accusation against the woman.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李崇 - A historical figure, possibly a high-ranking official or scholar.
  • 魏先生 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 李义琛 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 蒋恒 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 王璥 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 李杰 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 裴子云 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 郭正一 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 张楚金 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 董行成 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 张鷟 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 张松夀 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 苏无名 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 赵防 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 袁滋 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 袁安 - A historical figure, possibly a high-ranking official or scholar.
  • 严遵 - A historical figure, possibly a high-ranking official or scholar.
  • 楚王 - Refers to the king of Chu, a state during the Han dynasty.
  • 汉 - Refers to the Han dynasty, a significant period in Chinese history.
  • 楚相 - Refers to the chancellor of Chu, a high-ranking official in the state of Chu.
  • 刺史 - Refers to a governor or provincial administrator in ancient China.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence is elliptical and must be read as 'Li Zichang, in his governance, wanted to know the prisoner's situation by using camphor trees as people.'
  • This sequence must be read as 'digging the ground to make a pit, using reeds to make a wall, and placing wooden prisoners inside.'
  • This clause must be read as 'if the prisoner's crime is correct, the wooden prisoner does not move; if the prisoner is wronged and invades the wooden prisoner, the wooden prisoner moves out.'
  • This clause must be read as 'not knowing whether the prisoner's spirit is on the wooden figure, the movement of the wooden prisoner is due to the divine breath.'
  • This clause must be read as 'when the king of Chu was involved in a case and others were implicated, thousands of people were detained.'
  • This clause must be read as 'the cruelty and violence of Chu, the prisoners all falsely confessed, after three years, the case was still unresolved.'
  • This clause must be read as 'over a hundred people died from torture and imprisonment, the sky used intense drought to turn a thousand miles of land red.'
  • This clause must be read as 'An accepted his appointment and immediately took control of the reins and proceeded, once he arrived, he resolved the cases, and each person recorded their statements.'
  • This clause must be read as 'they were not the main conspirators but were implicated by the king, they were promptly released.'
  • This clause must be read as 'in one day, he saved the lives of a thousand people, at that time, it rained abundantly, the year was a great harvest.'
  • This clause must be read as 'while traveling, he heard a woman crying beside the road, but her voice was not sad, indicating that the woman's crying was unusual.'
  • This clause must be read as 'he asked her, and she said her husband had been burned to death, indicating the woman's explanation for her crying.'
  • This clause must be read as 'Zun ordered the officials to bring the body, indicating that Zun ordered the officials to bring the body.'
  • This clause must be read as 'he ordered someone to guard it, indicating that someone was assigned to watch over the body.'
  • This clause must be read as 'there should be something coming, and after a day, flies gathered around the head, indicating the presence of a supernatural phenomenon.'
  • This clause must be read as 'Zun ordered the body to be examined, and a metal cone was found through the top of the head, indicating the discovery of a metal cone in the head.'
  • This clause must be read as 'he questioned her about killing her husband with adultery, indicating the accusation against the woman.'

[2029]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 頓丘李崇陳留公诞之子 - Introduces Rong, indicating his lineage and noble status.
  • 髙祖时为兖州刺史 - Describes Rong's position during the reign of the founding emperor.
  • 兖州比多刼盗 - Describes the situation in the state of Yan, indicating a period of lawlessness.
  • 村置一楼 - Describes the construction of towers in villages.
  • 楼悬一鼓 - Describes the installation of drums on the towers.
  • 捶鼓乱击 - Describes the method of signaling.

  • 证词甚具姓氏无差 - Indicates that the testimony is detailed and the names are accurate. '证词' means 'testimony,' and '甚具' means 'very detailed.' '姓氏无差' means 'no discrepancy in names.'

  • 囚则大恐叩头见血如不胜其寃者 - Describes the prisoner's extreme fear and physical suffering. '大恐' means 'great fear,' and '叩头见血' means 'knocking heads until blood appears.'
  • 所盗幸多金宝锦防非农家所寘蓄者 - Indicates that the stolen items are valuable and not typical of a farmer's possessions. '所盗幸多金宝锦防' means 'the stolen items are fortunately many gold, treasures, and brocades,' and '非农家所寘蓄者' means 'not something a farmer would keep.'
  • 汝宜籍舍之产以辩之 - Suggests that the prisoner should register the property to prove his innocence. '籍舍之产' means 'register the property of the house,' and '以辩之' means 'to prove it.'
  • 遂详开所贮者且不虞东邻之越讼也 - Indicates that the prisoner detailed his belongings without considering that the neighbor might sue. '详开所贮者' means 'detailedly list what is stored,' and '不虞东邻之越讼' means 'without suspecting that the eastern neighbor would sue.'

'赵宰大喜' means 'the magistrate was very pleased,' and '再审其事' means 'to re-examine the case.' '何谓讳' means 'why conceal,' and '东邻所赎八百千' means 'the eastern neighbor redeemed eight hundred strings.' '引诉邻' means 'to bring the neighbor to testify,' and '寘之于法' means 'to punish according to the law.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '为寇江囚则朗呌泪随曰' is elliptical and must be read as a single scene where the prisoner is brought before the magistrate and weeps.
  • The phrase '稼穑之夫未尝舟楫' is a simple statement but must be read in the context of the prisoner's unfamiliarity with water travel.
  • The phrase '证词甚具姓氏无差' is a concise statement about the testimony being detailed and accurate.
  • The phrase '或言伪而坚则血肤取实' is a cause-and-effect statement describing the method of extracting the truth.
  • The phrase '囚则大恐叩头见血如不胜其寃者' is a vivid description of the prisoner's fear and suffering.
  • The phrase '所盗幸多金宝锦防非农家所寘蓄者' is a detailed description of the stolen items and their value.
  • The phrase '汝宜籍舍之产以辩之' is a suggestion to the prisoner about how to prove his innocence.
  • The phrase '遂详开所贮者且不虞东邻之越讼也' is a continuation of the prisoner's actions and the unexpected turn of events.
  • The long sentence '乃言稻若干斛庄客某甲等纳到者防绢若干疋家机所出者钱若干贯东邻赎契者银器若干件匠某锻成者' is a detailed list of items and their sources.
  • The long sentence '赵宰大喜即再审其事谓曰如果非寇江者何谓讳东邻所赎八百千遂引诉邻令其偶证于是慙惧失色祈死防前赵令梏往本土检付契书然后寘之于法' is a detailed description of the magistrate's actions and the final outcome of the case.

  • 公左右皆笑 - Means 'your attendants all laughed.'

  • 与公料理往呼驿长责曰 - 'I will handle this and go call the relay station master to reprimand him.'
  • 判官与纳言何别不与供给索杖来 - 'What is the difference between the prefecture official and the Censor-in-Chief? If you don't provide supplies, bring a stick.'
  • 驿长惶怖拜伏 - 'The relay station master was terrified and prostrated himself.'

  • 且放却驿将跪拜流汗狼狈而走 - 'Let's let the relay station master go, and he will prostrate himself, sweating and stumbling away.'
  • 娄目送之谓判官曰与公踬顿之矣 - 'Looking after him, Liu said to the prefecture official, 'I have tripped you up.''
  • 众皆怪叹其行事皆此 - 'Everyone was surprised and praised his actions as such.'
  • 浮休子曰司马徽刘寛无以加也 - 'Fuhuizi said that even Sima Hui and Liu Kuan could not surpass him.'

  • 宰相 - Refers to the prime minister or chancellor, a high-ranking official in the imperial government.

  • 司马徽刘寛 - Sima Hui and Liu Kuan were famous for their wisdom and virtue in ancient China.

  • 搆隳辱宜当其辜 - This phrase can be read as 'the act of disgracing or humiliating someone should be punished for their guilt.'
  • 中外翕然莫测公子广 - This phrase can be read as 'widespread agreement or consensus, both inside and outside a certain context, no one can measure or understand the depth of the gentleman's broadness (公子广).'
  • 亲仁里大啓其第里巷贩之人上至公子簮缨之士出入不问 - This phrase can be read as 'the opening of a large residence in a neighborhood known for its kindness and benevolence, where people from the streets and alleys who engage in trade, as well as high-ranking officials (公子簮缨之士), come and go without being questioned.'
  • 王夫人赵氏爱女方妆梳对镜 - This phrase can be read as 'a woman (王夫人赵氏) is dressing and combing her hair in front of a mirror.'
  • 公麾下将吏出鎭去及郎吏皆被召令汲水持帨视之不异仆 - This phrase can be read as 'the general's officers and officials are being sent out to different places, and even low-ranking servants are being summoned to fetch water and hold towels, all treated equally.'

  • 代宗不名呼为大臣天下以其身存亡为安危者殆二十年 - Emperor Daizong did not call him by name but referred to him as a minister, and the people considered his life and death as the safety and danger of the nation for nearly twenty years.
  • 挍中书令考二十四年权倾天下而朝不忌功盖一代而主不疑侈穷人欲而君子不罪富贵寿考繁衍安泰终始人伦之盛无缺焉 - He served as the Chief Secretary for twenty-four years, wielding immense power without arousing jealousy in the court, achieving great accomplishments without causing suspicion from the emperor, indulging in his desires without being blamed by the virtuous, and enjoying wealth, honor, longevity, and a prosperous family throughout his life, fulfilling all aspects of human relationships.
  • 卒年八十五【出谈宾录】 - He died at the age of eighty-five, as recorded in *Tanbinlu*.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 尚不使人迎候其情状 - Indicates that the situation was not yet known, so no one was sent to greet; 'how could it be predicted?'
  • 晋曰某奉诏为汴州节度使准敕赴任 - A formal statement meaning 'Jin said, 'I have been ordered by the imperial decree to be the military governor of Bianzhou, and I am going to take up my post.''
  • 何可妄为逗留 - A rhetorical question meaning 'how could one dare to linger or delay?'
  • 人皆忧其不测晋独恬然 - Means 'everyone was worried about the unpredictable situation, but Jin alone remained calm and composed.'
  • 来至汴州数十里邓惟恭方来迎候 - Means 'he came within several dozen miles of Bianzhou, and Deng Weigong just came to greet him.'
  • 俾其不下马既入仍委惟恭以军众 - Means 'he ordered him not to dismount, and after entering, he still entrusted the troops to Deng Weigong.'
  • 初万荣既逐刘士宁代为节度使委兵于惟恭 - Means 'initially, Wan Rong had driven out Liu Shining and took over as military governor, and entrusted the troops to Deng Weigong.'
  • 及疾甚李乃归朝廷惟恭自以当便代居其位 - Means 'when Wan Rong fell gravely ill, Li returned to the imperial court, and Deng Weigong thought he should naturally take over the position.'
  • 故不遣候吏以疑惧晋心冀其不敢进 - Means 'therefore, he did not send officials to greet Jin in order to intimidate him, hoping that Jin would not dare to proceed.'
  • 不虞晋之速至晋之速至留以近方迎 - Means 'he did not expect Jin to arrive so quickly, and Jin's quick arrival left him near the place where Deng Weigong was supposed to greet him.'
  • 然心常怏怏惟恭以骄盈慢法潜图不轨 - Means 'but Deng Weigong's heart was always discontented, and Deng Weigong, arrogant and overconfident, secretly plotted to act against the law.'
  • 配流岭南朝廷恐晋柔懦防以汝州刺史陆长 - Means 'he was exiled to Lingnan, and the imperial court feared that Jin was weak and timid, leading them to appoint Lu Chang, the governor of Ruzhou, to guard against him.'

  • 强亲府署 - Literally 'forced himself to visit the military governor's office,' meaning 'tried to meet the military governor.'
  • 愿一见焉 - Literally 'wished to see her once,' expressing Cui Jiao's desire to meet the maid.
  • 因寒食果出 - Literally 'on the occasion of the Cold Food Festival, she happened to go out,' indicating the maid's outing during the festival.
  • 连泣誓若山河 - Literally 'cried and vowed like mountains and rivers,' meaning 'cried and swore with great emotion.'
  • 崔生赠之以诗曰 - Literally 'Cui Jiao gave her a poem which said,' introducing the poem Cui Jiao wrote.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太平广记 - A collection of Chinese stories and legends compiled during the Song Dynasty.
  • 钦定四库全书 - An official compilation of books ordered by the Qing Dynasty, known as the Four Treasuries.
  • 贡举 - Refers to the imperial examination system in ancient China.
  • 进士科 - The highest level of the imperial examination, leading to the title of 'jinshi'.
  • 缙绅 - Refers to high-ranking officials or nobles.
  • 白衣公卿 - A metaphor for those who are not nobles but are highly respected in society.
  • 一品白衫 - Refers to a high-ranking official who is not yet formally appointed.
  • 三十老明经 - Indicates that being thirty years old is considered old for passing the Mingjing examination.
  • 五十少进士 - Indicates that being fifty years old is still considered young for passing the Jinshi examination.
  • 倜傥之才 - Refers to outstanding talent or exceptional ability.
  • 变通之术 - Refers to the ability to adapt and change strategies.
  • 荆聂之胆气 - Refers to the courage and bravery of Jing Ke and Nie Zheng, famous assassins from the Warring States period.
  • 仲由之武勇 - Refers to the martial prowess of Zilu (Zong You), a disciple of Confucius.
  • 子房之筹画 - Refers to the strategic planning and wisdom of Zhang Liang (Zi Fang), a famous strategist from the Han Dynasty.
  • 赚得英雄尽白头 - A metaphorical expression suggesting that the imperial examinations have led many talented individuals to age without achieving their goals.
  • 俊乂 - Refers to talented and capable individuals.
  • 终身为文 - Refers to dedicating one's life to writing or scholarship.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 隋大业中 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Yang of Sui, during which the imperial examination system was established.
  • 贞观永徽之际 - Refers to the reigns of Emperor Taizong (贞观) and Emperor Gaozong (永徽) of Tang, a period of prosperity and cultural flourishing.
  • 谢恩 - The act of expressing gratitude to the emperor after passing the examinations.
  • 期集 - Refers to the gathering of successful candidates after the examinations.
  • 过堂 - Refers to the ceremony where successful candidates are examined by the emperor or high officials.
  • 题名 - Refers to the act of inscribing one's name on the list of successful candidates.
  • 闗试 - Refers to additional examinations or tests conducted by provincial authorities.
  • 防集 - Refers to the gathering of candidates before the final examination.

  • 太平广记 - A collection of Chinese stories and legends compiled during the Song Dynasty.
  • 钦定四库全书 - An official compilation of books ordered by the Qing Dynasty, known as the Four Treasuries.
  • 太宗皇帝 - Refers to Emperor Taizong of Tang, also known as Li Shimin.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 人故争名常切为时所 - People naturally strive for fame, often to the point of being criticized by the times. The character '' (likely a typo or rare character) is interpreted as '批评' (criticism) based on context.
  • 都防谓之举塲通称谓之秀才 - Local officials refer to the examination venue as the place where '秀才' (scholar-officials) are selected.
  • 投刺谓之乡贡 - Presenting a name card is called '乡贡' (local recommendation).
  • 得第谓之前进士 - Passing the exam is called '前进士' (advanced scholar-officials).
  • 互相推敬谓之先辈 - Mutual respect and recommendation are called '先辈' (senior scholars).
  • 俱捷谓之同年 - Both passing is called '同年' (classmates in the same year).
  • 有司谓之座主 - Officials are called '座主' (main examiner).
  • 京兆府考而升者谓之等第 - Those promoted by the capital's administration after passing the exam are called '等第' (grades).
  • 外府不试而贡者谓之拔解 - Those recommended by local administrations without taking the exam are called '拔解' (special promotion).
  • 将试相保谓之合保 - Mutual guarantee before the exam is called '合保' (joint guarantee).
  • 群居而赋谓之私试 - Gathering to write essays is called '私试' (private exams).
  • 造请权要谓之闗节 - Visiting powerful officials is called '闗节' (bribery).
  • 激声价谓之还往 - Boosting reputation through reciprocal visits is called '还往' (reciprocal visits).
  • 既捷列姓名于慈恩寺塔谓之题名 - Listing names on the Great Wild Goose Pagoda after passing is called '题名' (inscribing names).
  • 大防于曲江亭子谓之曲江防 - The grand gathering at the Qujiang Pavilion is called '曲江防' (Qujiang Gathering).
  • 籍而入选谓之春闱 - Being registered and selected is called '春闱' (spring examination).
  • 不捷而醉饱谓之打毷氉 - Failing and celebrating with drunkenness is called '打毷氉' (celebrating failure).
  • 匿名造牓谓之无名子 - Posting anonymously is called '无名子' (anonymous person).
  • 退而肄业谓之过夏 - Retiring to study is called '过夏' (summer break).
  • 执业以出谓之夏课 - Studying and leaving is called '夏课' (summer assignments).
  • 挟藏入试谓之书防 - Bringing in hidden materials is called '书防' (cheating).
  • 此其大畧者也 - These are the general points.
  • 其风俗系于先达 - Customs are influenced by predecessors.
  • 其制置存于有司 - The system is maintained by officials.
  • 虽然贤者得其大者 - Although the wise obtain the greater things.
  • 故位极人臣常十有二三 - Therefore, they often reach the highest positions.
  • 登显列十有六七 - They often enter prominent ranks.
  • 而元鲁山张睢阳有焉 - And Yuan Lushan and Zhang Suichang are among them.
  • 刘辟元翛有焉 - And Liu Pi and Yuan Xiu are among them.
  • 出国史补 - Refers to a historical text, 'Guo Shi Bu' (《国史补》).

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 进士归礼部 - Refers to the process of imperial examination candidates being assigned to the Ministry of Rites for final assessment.
  • 俊秀等科 - Refers to various categories of the imperial examinations, emphasizing the selection of talented individuals.
  • 考功主之 - Means that the Ministry of Personnel is responsible for the examination.
  • 开元二十四年 - Refers to the twenty-fourth year of the Kaiyuan era during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 员外郎李昻 - Refers to Li Ang, an official holding the rank of Yiwailang (a junior official).
  • 性不容物 - Describes Li Ang's personality as intolerant or harsh.
  • 贡士 - Refers to candidates who have passed the provincial examinations and are eligible for the metropolitan examinations.
  • 文之美恶悉之矣 - Means that the quality of their essays is known to him.
  • 考校取检存乎至公 - Emphasizes that the examination must be conducted with utmost fairness.
  • 如有请托于人当悉落之 - Means that anyone found to have solicited favors or made requests should be disqualified.
  • 外舅 - Refers to a father-in-law.
  • 李权 - The name of a candidate.
  • 隂求昻瑕 - Means that Rui secretly sought out flaws in Li Ang's conduct.
  • 摘权章句小疵 - Means that Li Ang found minor faults in Rui's essay.
  • 牓于通衢以辱之 - Means that Li Ang publicly posted Rui's faults in a public place to humiliate him.
  • 礼尚往来 - An idiom meaning 'etiquette demands reciprocity' or 'there should be mutual courtesy.'
  • 瑜不掩瑕 - An idiom meaning 'a jade blemish should not hide a flaw,' emphasizing honesty and fairness.
  • 其词或有不典雅 - Means that some of his words might not be elegant or refined.
  • 向之言其意属我也 - Means that the previous statement was directed at me.
  • 权出谓众曰 - Means that Rui spoke to the group.
  • 权曰 - Means that Rui said.
  • 耳临清渭洗心向白云闲 - A poetic phrase that means 'listening to the clear Wei River and washing one's heart toward the idle white clouds.'
  • 今天子春秋鼎盛 - Means that the current emperor is in the prime of his life.
  • 揖譲 - Means 'to yield or give way,' often used in the context of offering a position.
  • 惶骇 - Means 'frightened or terrified.'
  • 不逊 - Means 'impolite or disrespectful.'
  • 遂下权吏 - Means that Rui was punished by being sent to the prison.
  • 初昻强愎不受嘱请 - Means that initially, Li Ang was stubborn and refused to accept any requests.
  • 及有势位求者莫不允从 - Means that when someone with power or status requested something, he would always comply.
  • 廷议 - Refers to the court's deliberation.
  • 省郎位轻 - Means that the rank of Shenglang (a junior official) was too low.
  • 不足以伏多士 - Means that it was not sufficient to command respect from many scholars.
  • 乃命礼部侍郎专知焉 - Means that they appointed a Shilang of the Ministry of Rites to take charge.

[2130]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 曰 - A particle used to introduce a statement or quote, often translated as 'said' or 'stated.'
  • 且异于骊龙采斯疎矣 - A complex phrase that can be broken down as follows: '且' - 'also' or 'moreover'; '异于' - 'different from'; '骊龙' - 'mythical black dragon,' often associated with great power or mystery; '采斯' - 'to gather' or 'to select,' and '斯' - 'this' or 'thus'; '疎矣' - 'sparse' or 'scattered,' possibly indicating a lack of density or concentration.
  • 白石又殊于老蚌割莫得之 - Another complex phrase: '白石' - 'white stone,' possibly a metaphor or reference to something pure or valuable; '殊于' - 'different from'; '老蚌' - 'old clam,' often used metaphorically to refer to something that has been long held or concealed; '割莫得之' - 'cannot be obtained by cutting,' suggesting that something is unattainable or hidden.
  • 楚大伏其精当 - '楚大' - likely a proper name or title; '伏其精当' - 'admired his precision and appropriateness,' indicating high regard for someone's work or judgment.
  • 遂夺正解头 - '遂' - 'therefore' or 'thus'; '夺' - 'to take away' or 'to deprive'; '正' - a proper name, likely a person's name or title; '解头' - 'interpretation' or 'explanation,' possibly referring to a scholarly or official role.
  • 后正自丞郎使判盐鐡 - '后' - 'later' or 'afterward'; '自' - 'from' or 'as'; '丞郎' - a title, likely referring to an official position; '使判盐鐡' - 'was sent to oversee salt and iron,' referring to administrative duties.
  • 俄而为植所据 - '俄而' - 'soon after' or 'shortly thereafter'; '为植所据' - 'was taken over by Zhi,' where '植' is a proper name.
  • 正以手札戏植 - '以' - 'with' or 'using'; '手札' - 'personal letter' or 'private note'; '戏' - 'to jest' or 'to mock'; '植' - a proper name, likely referring to the same person as '植' in the previous phrase.
  • 昔日华元已遭毒手 - '昔日' - 'in the past' or 'formerly'; '华元' - a proper name, likely referring to a historical figure; '已遭毒手' - 'has already been harmed' or 'has already suffered harm.'
  • 今来鹾务又中老拳 - '今来' - 'now' or 'in the present'; '鹾务' - 'salt affairs,' referring to administrative duties related to salt; '又中老拳' - 'has again been hit by an old fist,' a metaphorical expression suggesting repeated harm or difficulty.
  • 大中中纥干峻与魏争府元而纥干屈居其下 - '大中' - a proper name or title; '纥干峻' - a proper name; '与魏争府元' - 'competed with Wei for the position of head of the office,' where '魏' is a proper name; '而纥干屈居其下' - 'but Heda was placed below him,' indicating a hierarchical relationship.
  • 翌日暴卒 - '翌日' - 'the next day'; '暴卒' - 'died suddenly.'
  • 时峻父方镇南海 - '时' - 'at that time'; '峻父' - 'Junda's father'; '方镇南海' - 'was stationed in Nanhai,' referring to a military or administrative post.
  • 由是为无名子所谤 - '由是' - 'therefore' or 'as a result'; '为无名子所谤' - 'was slandered by an anonymous person,' indicating criticism or defamation.
  • 曰离南海之日应得数斤当北阙之前未销一捻 - '曰' - 'said' or 'stated'; '离南海之日' - 'when leaving Nanhai'; '应得数斤' - 'should have gained several pounds'; '当北阙之前' - 'in front of the northern gate,' likely a metaphorical reference to a place of power or authority; '未销一捻' - 'did not melt even a little,' possibly referring to something not being affected or changed.
  • 因此峻兄弟皆罢举 - '因此' - 'as a result'; '峻兄弟' - 'Junda and his brothers'; '皆罢举' - 'all were dismissed from their positions.'
  • 张又新时号张三头 - '张又新' - a proper name; '时号' - 'at that time was called'; '张三头' - 'Zhang San Tou,' a nickname or title.
  • 【进士状头宏词敕头京兆解头】 - '进士' - 'imperial scholar,' a title for those who passed the imperial exams; '状头' - 'top scorer' or 'first place winner'; '宏词' - 'grand words' or 'grand compositions,' referring to literary achievements; '敕头' - 'imperial decree' or 'imperial order'; '京兆解头' - 'head of the capital's examination,' referring to an administrative or scholarly position.

  • 崔敖知之旭日都堂始开盛气白主司若出白云起封中题请退为其所中卒愕然换之 - This sequence describes 崔敖's actions: 崔敖 knew about it, when the sun rose and the main hall opened, he confidently told the chief examiner if the question was 'White Cloud Rising in the Sealed Letter,' he asked to step back, but was caught by him, finally, he was surprised and changed the question.
  • 是嵗二崔俱防 - This clause states that that year, both 崔s passed the exam.
  • 彭伉湛贲俱州宜春人伉妻又湛姨也伉举进士擢第湛犹为县吏妻族为置贺宴皆官人名士伉居席之右一座尽倾湛至命饭于后阁甚无难色其妻忿然责之 - This long sentence describes the relationship and status of 彭伉 and 湛贲: 彭伉 and 湛贲 were both from Yizhou Yichun, 彭伉's wife was also 湛贲's aunt, 彭伉 passed the 进士 exam and was ranked high, 湛贲 was still a county official, his wife's family held a congratulatory banquet, all were officials and distinguished scholars, 彭伉 sat to the right of the seat, everyone in the room was impressed, when 湛贲 arrived, he was ordered to eat in the back hall, he showed no sign of difficulty, his wife angrily scolded him.
  • 男子不能自励窘辱如此复何为容感其言孜孜学业未数载一举登第伉常侮之其时伉方跨驴縦游于郊郛忽有家僮驰报湛郎及第伉失声而坠 - This long sentence describes 湛贲's reaction and 彭伉's fall: a man cannot motivate himself, to be humiliated like this, what else is there to do? she was moved by her words, diligently studied, after a few years, he passed the exam, 彭伉 often mocked him, at that time, 彭伉 was riding a donkey and wandering in the suburbs, suddenly, a servant ran to tell 彭伉 that 湛贲 had passed the exam, 彭伉 screamed and fell off the donkey.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • Liu Fen, a student of Yang Sifu, offended the eunuchs by speaking frankly, and they were especially angry.
  • Zhongwei Chou Shiliang said to Sifu, 'Why did you let this wind of Han be released by the state examination?'
  • Sifu, frightened, answered, 'In the past, when I passed the exams with Fen, it was not yet a wind.'
  • Xue Baosun liked to write large works and called himself 'Diamond Club.' During Taihe, there were no fewer than a thousand candidates. The doors of the high officials were filled with scrolls, and the cost was borne by the doorkeepers and maidservants. As a result, those who were easy-going said that Xue Baosun's scroll would get double the usual reward.
  • Jia Dao was not good at the imperial examination. Each time he took the exam, he folded a piece of paper and went around telling people, 'Ask the original masters to beg for one couplet at a time.'
  • Bi Yan passed the exam and walked with a few others, listening to the sounds at night when people were few, and for a long time, he heard nothing. Suddenly, he met someone throwing bones on the ground, and the dogs fought to get them. The later ones will surely carry them away.

  • 琮以久隨計不第窮悴甚 - Elliptical sentence meaning 'Cong had been following the imperial examination for a long time without success and was in dire straits.'
  • 妻族益相薄 - Describes the deteriorating relationship between Zhao Cong and his in-laws due to his lack of success.
  • 雖妻父母不能不然也 - Emphasizes the extent of the coldness from his in-laws.
  • 一日軍中髙防州郡謂之春設者 - Describes a significant military event, 'One day, during a high-ranking military parade in the spring, which was a grand display by the state.'
  • 大將家相率列棚以觀之 - Shows the social status and interest of the generals' families, 'The families of the generals lined up to watch it.'
  • 其妻雖貧不能無徃然所服故敝眾以帷隔絶之 - Highlights the social pressure and expectations placed on her, 'His wife, despite her poverty, could not avoid going, but she wore old clothes and was separated from the crowd by curtains.'
  • 設方酣廉使忽驰吏呼将 - Introduces a sudden and unexpected event, 'As the parade was in full swing, the censor suddenly sent a messenger to call the general.'
  • 將驚且惧既至廉使臨轩手持一书笑曰 - Describes the censor's unexpected appearance and demeanor, 'The general was both surprised and fearful. When he arrived, the censor stood on the platform, holding a book and smiling.'
  • 乃告之适报至已及第矣 - Reveals the good news about Zhao Cong's success, 'He then told him that the news just arrived saying he had passed the exam.'
  • 即授所持书乃牓也将遽以榜归 - Describes the immediate action taken upon receiving the news, 'He immediately handed over the book, which was the list of successful candidates. The general quickly took the list back.'
  • 呼曰赵及第矣 - Announces Zhao Cong's success, 'He shouted, 'Zhao Cong has passed the exam!''
  • 妻之族即撤去帷障相与同席竞以簪服而庆遗焉 - Describes the sudden change in attitude and behavior of Zhao Cong's in-laws, 'His wife's family immediately removed the curtains, sat together at the same table, and competed to offer congratulations and gifts.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 咸通中 - Temporal phrase meaning 'during the Xiantong period,' referring to the reign of Emperor Xianzong of Tang.
  • 进士车服僣差 - Refers to the excessive display of wealth and status by candidates for the jinshi examination, particularly in terms of their vehicles and clothing.
  • 不许乘马 - Indicates that it was forbidden for these candidates to ride horses, possibly due to a decree or custom.
  • 时塲中不减千人 - Means 'at the time, there were no less than a thousand people in the examination hall,' emphasizing the large number of participants.
  • 虽势可热手亦皆骑驴 - A colloquial expression suggesting that despite their potential for success, all candidates rode donkeys, implying a sense of humility or necessity.
  • 短袖长秋满九衢 - A poetic description of the scene, where '短袖' (short sleeves) refers to the clothing style, and '长秋' (long autumn) and '满九衢' (filling the nine streets) evoke a vivid image of the crowded and fashionable atmosphere.
  • 清痩儿郎犹自可 - Describes the appearance of the candidates as lean and thin, but still acceptable or admirable.
  • 就中愁杀郑昌图 - A sarcastic remark, indicating that among all these candidates, Zheng Changtu was particularly distressed, likely due to his own circumstances or appearance.
  • 典眉州 - Means 'governor of Meizhou,' indicating a high-ranking official position.
  • 乡役充防仆 - Refers to a local laborer serving as a guard or attendant.
  • 薄涉艺文 - Means 'lightly involved in literature and arts,' suggesting a modest engagement with scholarly pursuits.
  • 赋咏雅有意思 - Indicates that the person has a refined taste and talent in poetry and literature.
  • 致书谢新人 - Means 'to send a letter of thanks to the new examiners,' showing a polite gesture of gratitude.
  • 三条烛下虽阻文闱 - Refers to the examination process, where '三条烛' (three candles) symbolizes the strict examination conditions, and '虽阻文闱' (though hindered by the examination) suggests the challenges faced.
  • 数仞墙邉幸同恩地 - A metaphorical expression, where '数仞墙' (a wall several feet high) and '幸同恩地' (fortunately sharing the same favor) imply a sense of mutual benefit and connection.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 进士 - Refers to the highest level of the imperial examination, which was highly prestigious.
  • 车服 - Refers to vehicles and clothing, which were significant indicators of social status in ancient China.
  • 僣差 - Means 'excessive' or 'overstepping,' often used to criticize those who display too much wealth or status.
  • 骑驴 - Riding a donkey, which was a common mode of transportation for scholars and officials, especially during times of financial constraint or modesty.
  • 短袖长秋 - Describes a specific fashion trend, where '短袖' (short sleeves) and '长秋' (long autumn) refer to the style of clothing worn during the autumn season.

  • 清痩儿郎 - Describes a lean and thin appearance, often associated with scholarly or ascetic individuals.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 率多退落 - Means 'mostly fail' or 'frequently fall short,' referring to the high rate of failure among candidates.
  • 屈人颇多 - Means 'many people are wronged' or 'many are unjustly disadvantaged,' highlighting the unfairness in the examination process.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 唐朝自广明庚子之乱甲辰天下大荒 - Compound expression referring to the chaos and famine that began in the Guangming era (880 CE) and continued until the Jia Chen era (884 CE).
  • 车驾再幸岐梁 - Refers to the emperor's repeated visits to Qiliang, indicating the political turmoil and instability during this period.
  • 饥馑相望 - Means 'famine followed famine,' emphasizing the severity and duration of the food shortages.
  • 郡国率不以贡士为意 - Means 'counties and kingdoms generally did not take the submission of scholars seriously,' highlighting the neglect of scholarly talent.
  • 江西节帅钟传 - Refers to Zhong Chuan, the military governor of Jiangxi, who rose to power through military service.
  • 起于义聚 - Means 'rose from a gathering of righteous people,' indicating Zhong Chuan's rise to power through a coalition of like-minded individuals.
  • 奄有疆土 - Means 'seized territory,' describing Zhong Chuan's expansion of his domain.
  • 充庭述职 - Means 'fulfilled his duties in the imperial court,' indicating Zhong Chuan's role as a loyal vassal.
  • 为诸侯表式 - Means 'became a model for the feudal lords,' highlighting Zhong Chuan's exemplary leadership.
  • 孜孜以荐贤为急务 - Means 'diligently made recommending virtuous men his top priority,' emphasizing Zhong Chuan's commitment to talent cultivation.
  • 州里白丁片文只字 - Refers to commoners with even a small amount of education, indicating the broad range of people Zhong Chuan sought to support.
  • 拳拳然有喜色 - Means 'showed a sincere and joyful expression,' describing Zhong Chuan's genuine enthusiasm for scholarly achievements.
  • 大防以饯之 - Means 'held a farewell banquet to see them off,' indicating the importance of the scholars' departure.
  • 筐箧之外率皆资以桂玉 - Means 'outside of their basic needs, they were provided with expensive items,' emphasizing the generous support given to scholars.
  • 解元三十万解副二十万其余皆不减十万 - Refers to the financial rewards given to top scholars, with the first-place winner receiving 300,000 coins, the second-place winner receiving 200,000 coins, and others receiving at least 100,000 coins.
  • 垂三十载此志未尝稍息 - Means 'for nearly thirty years, this commitment never wavered,' highlighting the long-term dedication to talent cultivation.
  • 时举子者以公卿闗节不逺千里而求首荐 - Means 'scholars traveled far and wide to seek the recommendation of high officials,' indicating the importance of official endorsement.
  • 岁常不下数辈 - Means 'each year, several scholars were recommended,' highlighting the consistent support for talent.
  • 卢文焕光化二年状元及第 - Refers to Lu Wenhuan, who passed the imperial examination as the top scholar in the Guanghua era (895 CE).
  • 颇以防醵为急务 - Means 'made preventing gambling a top priority,' indicating Lu Wenhuan's efforts to maintain social order.
  • 常府开防同年皆患贫无以致之一旦绐以游齐国公亭子 - Means 'one day, he tricked them into going to the Pavilion of Duke Qi by pretending to organize a trip,' indicating Lu Wenhuan's cleverness in managing his contemporaries.
  • 居四年璨登庸文涣忧戚日加 - Means 'after four years, Liu Cuan rose to power, and Wen Huan's worries increased day by day,' highlighting the political tension.
  • 药不厥疾弗瘳 - Means 'If medicine doesn't cure the disease, it's useless,' indicating the futility of Wen Huan's efforts.
  • 出摭言 - Refers to the book *Zhiyan* (Notes on the Selection of Scholars), a historical record of the imperial examinations.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐朝 - Refers to the Tang Dynasty (618-907 CE), a period of great cultural and political significance in Chinese history.
  • 广明庚子之乱甲辰天下大荒 - Refers to the An Lushan Rebellion (755-763 CE) and the subsequent chaos, which began in the Guangming era (880 CE) and continued until the Jia Chen era (884 CE).
  • 车驾再幸岐梁 - Refers to the emperor's visits to Qiliang, indicating the political instability and the need for the emperor to seek refuge.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 光化二年赵光逢放栁璨及第 - This sentence indicates that Zhao Guangfeng passed the imperial examination with Liu Can in the second year of the Guanghua era.
  • 光逢后三年不迁 - This phrase means that Zhao Guangfeng did not receive a promotion for three years after passing the examination.
  • 时璨自内庭大拜 - This phrase means that Liu Can was promoted to a high position in the imperial court.
  • 光逢始以左丞徵入 - This phrase means that Zhao Guangfeng was finally summoned and appointed as the Left Chancellor.
  • 未防璨坐罪诛死 - This phrase means that Liu Can was later found guilty and executed.
  • 光逢膺大用居重地十余岁 - This phrase means that Zhao Guangfeng held a significant position for more than ten years.
  • 上表乞骸守司空致仕 - This phrase means that Zhao Guangfeng submitted a memorial requesting retirement and was appointed as the Sikuang before retiring.
  • 复徴拜上相 - This phrase means that Zhao Guangfeng was again summoned and appointed as the prime minister.
  • 卢延让光化三年登第 - This phrase means that Lu Yanrang passed the imperial examination in the third year of the Guanghua era.
  • 先是延让师薛能为诗词 - This phrase means that Lu Yanrang studied under Xue Neng in poetry and prose.
  • 诗词意入癖时人多笑之 - This phrase means that his poetry and prose were deeply influenced by Xue Neng, and people often laughed at him.
  • 吴融向为侍御史出官峡中 - This phrase means that Wu Rong previously served as a censor and served in the Xiang region.
  • 延让时薄游荆渚贫无卷轴未遑贽谒 - This phrase means that Lu Yanrang travelled lightly to Jingzhou, was poor, had no books or scrolls, and had no time to present gifts and pay respects.
  • 防融表弟滕籍者偶得延让百篇 - This phrase means that Wu Rong's cousin, Teng Ji, accidentally obtained Lu Yanrang's hundred poems.
  • 融既览大奇之且曰此无他贵语不寻常耳 - This phrase means that Wu Rong found them very extraordinary after reading them and said that there was nothing else; it's just that his words were not ordinary.
  • 称之于府主成汭时故相张公职于是县常以延让为笑端 - This phrase means that Wu Rong commended Lu Yanrang to Cheng Rui, the governor, and at that time, the former chancellor Zhang Gong held an official position in the county and often took Lu Yanrang as the subject of ridicule.
  • 及融言之咸所改观 - This phrase means that when Wu Rong spoke about him, everyone changed their view.
  • 由是大获举粮延让深所感激 - This phrase means that therefore, he gained much support and resources, and Lu Yanrang was deeply grateful.
  • 因循竟未相面值融赴急征寻入内庭孜孜于公卿间称誉不已 - This phrase means that they remained in contact but never met face-to-face. When Wu Rong was urgently summoned to the imperial court, he diligently among the high officials and kept praising him without end.
  • 光化戊午岁来自襄之南一见如旧相识呜咽流涕于是攘臂成之矣 - This phrase means that in the year of the Wuxu in the Guanghua era, Lu Yanrang came from the south of Xiang, met Wu Rong as if they had known each other for a long time, shed tears of sorrow, and thus they became close friends.

  • 其有非士族及假冒者多不见录署 - Means that those who are not of noble lineage or who falsely claim such status are often not recorded.
  • 云相州僧昙刚撰 - Indicates that the work is attributed to a monk named Tan Gang from Xiangzhou.
  • 后栁冲亦明族姓 - Suggests that later, Liu Chong also became knowledgeable about family lineages.
  • 中宗朝为相州刺史 - Refers to the period when Liu Chong served as the governor of Xiangzhou during the reign of Emperor Zhongzong.
  • 询问旧老 - Means to consult with elderly people or long-time residents.
  • 自隋已来不闻有僧昙刚 - Indicates that since the Sui dynasty, there has been no record of a monk named Tan Gang.
  • 葢惧嫉于时故其名氏 - Suggests that the monk may have been feared or envied during his time, leading to the suppression of his name.
  • 初李峤与李回秀同在庙堂 - Refers to the initial period when Li Qiao and Li Huixiu were both in the imperial court.
  • 奉诏为兄弟 - Means they were ordered by the emperor to become brothers.
  • 西祖王璋与信安王袆同产 - Indicates that Wang Zhang and Xin'an Wang Hui were born to the same family.
  • 故赵郡陇西二族昭穆不定 - Suggests that the lineage of the Zhao and Longxi clans was unclear.
  • 一防之中或孙为祖或祖为孙 - Indicates that within a single generation, the relationship between grandfather and grandson could be reversed.
  • 张说好求山东婚姻 - Refers to Zhang Yue's preference for marrying into families from Shandong.
  • 当时皆恶之 - Means that at the time, everyone disliked this practice.
  • 及后与张氏亲者乃为申明四姓 - Suggests that later, those who married into the Zhang family were recognized as part of the four prominent families.
  • 郑氏不离荥阳 - Indicates that the Zheng family remained in荥yang.
  • 岗头卢泽底李土门崔皆为鼎甲 - Refers to the Lu, Li, Cui, and other families being considered the most prestigious.
  • 杨氏自杨震号闗西孔子 - Refers to the Yang family, starting with Yang Zhen, being known as the 'Confucius of the Western Pass.'
  • 于潼闗亭 - Means that Yang Zhen was buried at the Tongguan Pavilion.
  • 至今七百余年 - Indicates that this has been the case for over seven hundred years.
  • 子孙犹在阌乡故宅 - Suggests that the descendants still live in the old house in Wenxiang.
  • 天下一家而已 - Means that the entire family is united.
  • 李尚书益有宗人庶子同名俱出于姑臧公 - Refers to the fact that there were two individuals named Li Yi, one being a relative of the other, both descending from the same ancestor.
  • 时人谓尚书为文章李益庶子为门户李益 - Indicates that people called the official Li Yi 'Li Yi the Scholar' and the other Li Yi 'Li Yi the Gatekeeper.'
  • 而尚书亦兼门第焉 - Suggests that the official Li Yi also had a distinguished family background.
  • 尝姻族间有礼防 - Refers to a time when there was a family gathering.
  • 大堪笑今日局席两个坐头总是李益 - Means that it was amusing that at the gathering, two people sitting at the head of the table were both named Li Yi.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 何方穏便 - Which direction is convenient? or Which path is suitable? A question used to assess the candidate's preferences or circumstances.
  • 注与吴复 - Assigned to Wu and Fu, referring to the assignment of officials to specific regions or roles.
  • 亲老先住江南 - My parents are old and they live in southern China first, indicating a reason for the candidate's preference.
  • 陇右论者莫测其意 - Those who commented in the Western Regions could not fathom his intentions, suggesting that the candidate's decisions were mysterious or difficult to understand.
  • 信都人希河朔 - A person from Xindu who wished for the northern regions, indicating a candidate's desire for a particular region.
  • 绐云愿得江淮 - Deceived by saying he wanted the Jiang and Huai regions, suggesting that the candidate lied about his preferences.
  • 大被选人绐言欺之 - Was greatly deceived by the candidates' deceitful words, indicating that the official was manipulated by the candidates.
  • 贵公子四海人物未能委练 - A nobleman who could not thoroughly evaluate the talents of people from all over the country, suggesting a lack of competence in assessing candidates.
  • 深抑势贵及亲党 - He deeply suppressed the powerful and influential as well as his relatives and party members, indicating a deliberate avoidance of favoritism.
  • 以避嫌 - To avoid suspicion, suggesting that the official took this action to maintain impartiality.
  • 时论讥之 - The contemporary critics mocked him, indicating that the official's actions were criticized.
  • 知选凡所铨综 - In charge of the selection process, he comprehensively evaluated all candidates, indicating a thorough and fair evaluation.
  • 时称允惬 - Was highly praised at the time, suggesting that the official's work was well-received.
  • 独知事 - Alone in charge of the selection process, indicating that the official was solely responsible for the evaluation.
  • 赐金镜一面以表清鉴 - Awarded a golden mirror to symbolize his clear judgment, indicating a recognition of the official's fairness and wisdom.
  • 好汲引寒俊 - Fond of promoting talented but poor individuals, indicating a preference for merit-based promotion.
  • 表荐任希古髙智周郭正一王义方孟利真等十余人 - Recommended Ren Xigu, Gao Zhi Zhou, Guo Zhengyi, Wang Yifang, Meng Lizhen, and over ten others, listing specific individuals who were promoted.
  • 时论称美 - Was praised by contemporary critics, indicating that the official's actions were well-received.

  • 排斥之 - Excluded them, indicating that the official was unfair in his evaluations.
  • 为者夏侯彪所讼 - Was sued by Xiahou Biao, who was in charge of the selection process, indicating that the official faced legal challenges.
  • 御史中丞郎余庆弹奏免官 - The Chief Censor Lang Yuqing impeached him and he was dismissed from office, indicating that the official was removed from his position.

  • 唐皎除吏部侍郎常引人入铨问何方穏便 — This sentence is elliptical and must be read as a single action: 'Tang Jiao, appointed as the Deputy Minister of Personnel, frequently brought people into the selection process and asked which direction was convenient.'
  • 或云其家在蜀乃注与吴复 — This sentence is elliptical and must be read as a single action: 'Some said his family was in Shu, so he was assigned to Wu and Fu.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐景龙年中 - The Jinglong era (707-710 AD) was marked by political turmoil and the brief restoration of the Tang dynasty after the Zhou dynasty's usurpation.
  • 尚书宋璟 - Song Jing was a renowned official known for his integrity and opposition to corruption.

  • 残明经进士 - The imperial examinations were a crucial means of selecting officials, and the candidates for these exams were highly respected.

  • 天下孝庑秀才茂异 - The outstanding scholars and talented individuals of the empire were a source of pride and a symbol of the state's intellectual capital.
  • 雷同与官 - The indiscriminate appointment of officials was a hallmark of the Zhou regime's corrupt practices.
  • 市道屠沽 - Merchants and butchers were considered low-status occupations, and their elevation to official positions was seen as a sign of moral decline.
  • 亡命不轨 - Fugitives and lawbreakers were given positions of power, further undermining the legitimacy of the regime.
  • 封侯 - The bestowal of titles of marquis was a significant honor, but when granted indiscriminately, it lost its value.
  • 太府之铜 - The copper reserves of the Imperial Treasury were essential for casting official seals, and their depletion symbolized the regime's financial and administrative decay.
  • 白版侯 - Marquises with blank or unsealed titles were a sign of the regime's inability to maintain proper records and procedures.
  • 朝防之服貂者大半 - The prevalence of sable fur robes in the court was a symbol of luxury and corruption.
  • 謡云 - The saying 'when the sable fur robes ran out, they were replaced with dog tails' was a metaphor for the decline in quality and standards.
  • 无道之朝 - The Zhou regime is described as an immoral and tyrannical regime, characterized by corruption and moral decay.
  • 出朝野佥载 - *Chao Ye Qian Zai* is a historical text that provides a detailed account of the events and conditions of the time.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 桞辟知甲库始白执政于都堂集八座丞郎焚之 - Describes how Liu Bi, the head of the treasury, reported the matter to the chief officials, who then gathered and burned the documents.
  • 省桥 - Refers to a bridge in the government offices, likely symbolic of the connections and interactions within the bureaucracy.
  • 拗项桥 - A colloquial term for the bridge, suggesting a physical characteristic or a metaphorical meaning.
  • 侍御史及殿中乆次者至此必拗项南望 - Describes a custom where certain officials would turn their heads to look south upon reaching this bridge, possibly as a gesture of respect or tradition.
  • 南宫也都堂南门道东有古槐垂隂至广 - Refers to an old willow tree with extensive shade near the southern gate of the palace, a significant landmark.
  • 音声树 - A colloquial name for the old willow tree, suggesting it had a special sound or was associated with music.
  • 氷【去声】防 - A term used by the Censorate, likely referring to the cold and clear nature of the tree.
  • 秘书省 - Refers to the Ministry of Rites, responsible for managing the imperial library and archives.
  • 唐初秘书省唯主写书贮掌勘校而已 - Describes the initial function of the Ministry of Rites, which was limited to copying and storing books.
  • 门可张罗 - A metaphor indicating the lack of activity or importance of the ministry.
  • 权贵子弟及好利夸侈者率不好此职 - Indicates that influential and wealthy individuals generally did not favor this position.
  • 流俗以监为宰相病坊少监为给事中中书舍人病坊丞及著作为尚书病坊秘书及著作左为监察御史病坊 - Describes the hierarchical progression of positions within the bureaucracy, with each level being less desirable than the previous one.
  • 言从职不任繁剧者当改入此省 - Suggests that those who found their current positions too demanding should consider transferring to the Ministry of Rites.
  • 然其职在图史非复喧卑 - Indicates that despite its lower status, the Ministry of Rites was still important for its role in managing historical records and literature.
  • 好学君子厌于趋竞者亦求为此职焉 - Suggests that scholars who were tired of the competitive nature of other positions might seek employment here.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 武后 - Refers to Empress Wu Zetian, who ruled the Tang Dynasty from 690 to 705 CE.

  • 河中府 - Refers to Hezhong Prefecture, another significant administrative region during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 天祐 - Refers to the year 904 CE, marking the end of the Tang Dynasty and the beginning of the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '鱼袋朝仪鱼袋之餙唯金银二等至武后乃改五品以铜中宗反正从旧' is complex and elliptical, describing the changes in the materials used for fish badges and the restoration of the old system.
  • The long, continuous sentence '京兆府判司时云两法士此两防事多东士曹防时号为念珠防葢判案一百八道西士曹防为莎防防前有莎周廻可十五歩京兆府时云不立两县令不坐两少尹两县引马到府门传门而报两尹入防大尹亦到防不得候两尹坐后出不得两尹立后出其京兆府县之重亦表大尹之尊京兆府掾曹时人云倚国省郎河中府司録防亦有緑莎昔好事者相承常溉灌天祐已后为不好事者除之' describes various administrative practices and customs in Jingzhao Prefecture, including the roles of different officials and the maintenance of a grassy area.
